food_subgroup: Mixed salad with leafy greens and raw vegetables

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FOODON_00002539 is the closest parent for a generic mixed salad food product. No exact code exists for a customisable mesclun-based salad.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers salads and similar products; used as closest parent as no exact code exists for a customisable mixed-vegetable salad.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226529007 is the closest parent concept for a generic salad food item.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard salad bowl serving of approximately 200 g, comprising mesclun leaves, cherry tomatoes, broccoli florets, and sliced carrots, as typically served at a Singapore café.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Mixed Salad Greens (raw) and individual raw vegetable components (cherry tomatoes, broccoli, carrots); composite estimate weighted by typical salad composition
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry exists for this customisable café salad. Nutrient values are estimated as a weighted composite of USDA FoodData Central entries for raw mesclun/mixed salad greens (FDC ID 787775), raw cherry tomatoes (FDC ID 170457), raw broccoli (FDC ID 170379), and raw carrots (FDC ID 170393), using an approximate weight ratio of 50% mesclun, 20% cherry tomatoes, 15% broccoli, 15% carrots. No dressing is included as the input description does not specify one.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — mixed salad vegetables and raw vegetable analogues
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific customisable café salad. GI of 32 is an analogous estimate based on published GI values for raw mixed salad greens and non-starchy vegetables (GI range 15–38) from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2021) international GI tables. All component vegetables are low-GI non-starchy foods. Glycemic load per 200 g serving calculated as (32 × 4.8 × 2.0) / 100 = 3.1 g, confirming very low GL.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (~28 mg per 100 g) and high potassium (~280 mg per 100 g) without added dressing
hypertension.guidance: This undressed salad is highly suitable for individuals with hypertension due to its very low sodium and favourable potassium content; patients should be advised to choose low-sodium dressings if any are added.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (~32) and low glycemic load (~3.1 per serving), with high dietary fibre supporting postprandial glucose control
type2_diabetes.guidance: This low-GI, high-fibre salad is well-suited for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes as it produces a minimal postprandial glucose response; patients should monitor any added dressings or toppings that may increase carbohydrate and sugar load.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Very low saturated fat (~0.05 g per 100 g), zero cholesterol, and high antioxidant micronutrient content (vitamin C, vitamin A, folate)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This salad is cardioprotective in its undressed form, providing negligible saturated fat and cholesterol alongside antioxidant vitamins and folate that support endothelial health; patients should avoid high-fat creamy dressings.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Create Your Salad is a customisable fresh salad served at The Tree Cafe (Funan, Singapore) and similar Western-style cafés in Singapore. As described, it comprises a base of mesclun salad mix — a blend of young tender leaves including rocket, mizuna, radicchio, and oak leaf — combined with cherry tomatoes, raw broccoli florets, and sliced carrots. The dish is served cold and raw, with no cooking applied to any component. The texture is crisp and refreshing, with a mild bitterness from the mesclun leaves balanced by the sweetness of cherry tomatoes and carrots. Nutritionally, this undressed salad is very low in calories (~28 kcal per 100 g), low in fat and saturated fat, and provides meaningful dietary fibre (~2.1 g per 100 g), vitamin C (~28 mg per 100 g), vitamin A (~120 mcg RAE per 100 g), folate (~55 mcg DFE per 100 g), and potassium (~280 mg per 100 g). Its glycemic index is low (~32) and glycemic load per standard 200 g serving is approximately 3.1, making it highly suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease. The dish is naturally vegan, gluten-free, dairy-free, and halal. It is priced at SGD 8.50 at the source restaurant. Clinically, this salad represents an excellent dietary choice for weight management, blood glucose control, and cardiovascular risk reduction, provided no high-calorie or high-sodium dressings are added.
common_user_queries: Is this salad good for diabetics?
How many calories in a garden salad?
What is the sodium content of a plain salad?
Can I eat this salad if I have high blood pressure?
What nutrients are in broccoli and carrot salad?

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry exists for this specific customisable café salad in any approved primary source. Values are estimated as a weighted composite of USDA FoodData Central entries for the four named raw vegetable components. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for non-starchy raw vegetables. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component foods are well-characterised in USDA FoodData Central and the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are composite estimates based on USDA FoodData Central data for raw mesclun/mixed salad greens, cherry tomatoes, broccoli, and carrots using an approximate 50:20:15:15 weight ratio. No dressing is included as none is specified in the input description. 2. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero as all components are raw plant foods with naturally zero trans fat content; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. 3. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 — naturally zero as all components are plant-based foods. 4. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 — naturally zero as this is a plant-only food with no animal-derived ingredients. 5. vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 — naturally zero for raw vegetables not exposed to UV treatment; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. 6. monounsaturated_fat_g (0.02), polyunsaturated_fat_g (0.12), potassium_mg (280.0), calcium_mg (38.0), iron_mg (0.8), vitamin_c_mg (28.0), vitamin_a_mcg_rae (120.0), vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg (0.06), vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg (0.07), vitamin_b3_niacin_mg (0.5), vitamin_b6_mg (0.12), folate_mcg_dfe (55.0), vitamin_e_mg (0.8), phosphorus_mg (42.0), magnesium_mg (16.0), zinc_mg (0.3), selenium_mcg (0.5), water_g (92.0) are all estimated from USDA FoodData Central composite. 7. The cuisine tag in the input is listed as 'Coffee' which appears to reflect the café category on GrabFood rather than the culinary origin of the dish; the dish is classified as Western café-style. 8. GI set to 32 as an analogous estimate for raw non-starchy mixed salad vegetables per University of Sydney GI Database; gi_category is low. GL per 200 g serving = (32 × 4.8 × 2.0) / 100 = 3.1.
